title: Write SQL expressions to join multi-source data

blocks.content:
Welcome to the Grafana learning path that walks you through writing SQL expressions to JOIN data from multiple queries.

SQL expressions let you run server-side SQL against query results. Each query's RefID (A, B, C) becomes a table name in your SQL, so you can write standard JOINs across data from different backends. This path guides you through the full workflow: building base queries, adding a SQL expression, writing a JOIN, and handling NULL values with COALESCE.

## Here's what to expect

When you complete this path, you'll be able to:

- Build base queries from different data sources and hide them from visualization
- Add a SQL expression and inspect data shape with `SELECT * FROM A`
- Write an INNER JOIN across two queries using RefIDs as table names
- Use COALESCE to handle NULL values in joined results
- Validate SQL expression output in Table view

## Before you begin

Before you write SQL expressions, ensure you have:

- A Grafana Cloud account. To create one, refer to [Grafana Cloud](https://grafana.com/signup/cloud/connect-account).
- Basic familiarity with SQL syntax (SELECT, JOIN, WHERE).
- The `sqlExpressions` feature toggle enabled. In Grafana Cloud, this is enabled by default. For self-hosted instances, refer to [Feature toggles](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/setup-grafana/configure-grafana/feature-toggles/).

This path uses the built-in Grafana (TestData) data source, so no external data source configuration is required.

## Troubleshooting

If you get stuck, we've got your back! Where appropriate, troubleshooting information is just a click away.

## More to explore

We understand you might want to explore other capabilities not strictly on this path. We'll provide you opportunities where it makes sense.
